tree.setup: A shopping mall entrance, early evening. You've tripped on a loose tile and scraped your knee. Security guard Lao Deng rushes over with a first-aid kit that he keeps impeccably organized -- bandages sorted by size, antiseptic wipes in a neat row. He treats every minor injury with the gravity of an emergency room surgeon.
tree.setup_zh: 商场入口，傍晚。你被松动的地砖绊倒，膝盖擦伤了。保安老邓拿着急救箱跑过来——绷带按大小分好，消毒巾整整齐齐。他对每个小伤都像急诊医生一样认真。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ méi shì ba? wǒ kànkan!
turns.text_en: Are you OK? Let me see!

options.text_pinyin: wǒ shuāi dǎo le, xīgài yǒudiǎn téng.
options.text_en: I fell down and my knee hurts a little.
options.feedback: Lao Deng kneels down and examines the scrape with the focus of an art restorer assessing a damaged painting.「不严重，我帮你擦一下。」摔倒 is 'to fall down' -- useful when you need it.

options.text_pinyin: méi shì méi shì, bú yàojǐn.
options.text_en: It's nothing, don't worry about it.
options.feedback: Lao Deng is not convinced. He has already opened the first-aid kit and will not be closing it without treating something. 不要紧 means 'it doesn't matter' -- good for brushing off concern.

options.text_en: Do we need to call an ambulance?
options.feedback: Lao Deng looks at your scraped knee, then back at you, with the measured patience of someone who has had this exact conversation before.「不用不用，我有创可贴。」It's a scrape.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 来，坐这里。我帮你贴个创可贴。
turns.text_pinyin: lái, zuò zhèlǐ. wǒ bāng nǐ tiē ge chuāngkětiē.
turns.text_en: Here, sit down. I'll put a band-aid on it.

options.text_pinyin: xièxie nǐ! nǐ rén zhēn hǎo.
options.feedback: Lao Deng waves it off modestly, but straightens his uniform a little.「小事小事。」你人真好 is a warm compliment -- 'you're really a good person.'
Lao Deng shakes his head firmly. This is literally his job and also his calling.「不麻烦！」麻烦你了 is a polite way to acknowledge someone's effort.

options.text_pinyin: nà ge dì zhuān tài wēixiǎn le!
options.text_en: That floor tile is too dangerous!
options.feedback: Lao Deng nods gravely and pulls out a small notebook. He writes down the location of the tile. He will report this. He reports everything. 危险 means 'dangerous.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo le! zǒu lù xiǎoxīn yìdiǎn a!

tree.cultural_note: Security guards (保安, bǎo'ān) in Chinese malls and residential compounds often go beyond their job description -- giving directions, lending umbrellas, and providing basic first aid. 创可贴 (chuāngkětiē) is the Chinese word for band-aid.
